title: Routine inspectors and testers
description: Operate, install, adjust, and maintain integrated computer/communications systems, consoles, simulators, and other data acquisition, test, and measurement instruments and equipment, which are used to launch, track, position, and evaluate air and space vehicles. May record and interpret test data. Operate, test, maintain, or adjust unmanned, automated, servomechanical, or electromechanical equipment. May operate unmanned submarines, aircraft, or other equipment to observe or record visual information at sites such as oil rigs, crop fields, buildings, or for similar infrastructure, deep ocean exploration, or hazardous waste removal. May assist engineers in testing and designing robotics equipment. Build, install, test, or maintain robotic equipment or related automated production systems. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Discard inferior or defective products or foreign matter, and place acceptable products in containers for further processing.
Place products in containers according to grade and mark grades on containers, and record grade or identification numbers on tags or sheets.
Grade and sort products according to factors such as color, species, length, and quality to ensure correct processing and usage.
Weigh products or estimate their weight, visually or by feel, and ensure proper installation of test equipment or components.
Build, install, test, or maintain robotic devices, systems, and instrumentation for various applications.
Analyze performance of vehicles or components redesigned to increase fuel efficiency or testing vehicles that use alternative fuels.
Draft detail drawings or sketches for testing or fabrication purposes, and prepare specifications or layouts for machines or systems.
Perform or execute manual or automated tests of automotive systems or components, evaluating performance, strength, and stress response.
Document test results using various tools and analyze test data for automotive systems or robotic assemblies, recommending design improvements as needed.
Inspect, diagnose, maintain, and operate test setups and equipment to detect malfunctions and make necessary repairs or adjustments.
Read and interpret blueprints, schematics, and specifications to determine methods and sequences of assembly or installation.
Test performance of electromechanical assemblies and robotic systems using multiple instruments to ensure specifications are met.
Install, program, or repair robotic systems, including components like controllers and sensors, and document procedures and results.
Assist engineers in the design, configuration, or application of robotic systems, and modify computer-controlled robot movements as necessary.
Inspect parts for surface defects and verify part dimensions to ensure conformance to specifications.
Collect or select samples for testing or for use as models and prepare inspection schedules or other documentation records.
Evaluate the efficiency and reliability of industrial robotic systems or automated processes, making adjustments to enhance output.
Monitor production operations or equipment to ensure conformance to specifications and take corrective actions when necessary.
Analyze test data, making necessary computations to determine test results and support quality control or assurance procedures.
Prepare written documentation of tests and recommend necessary corrective actions based on inspection results.
Train others on installing, using, or maintaining robotic systems, ensuring proper practices and procedures are followed.
Maintain service records of robotic equipment or automated production systems.
Train customers or other personnel to install, use, or maintain robots.
Record inspection or test data, such as weights, temperatures, or grades, and quantities inspected or graded.
Inspect work progress or construction sites to verify safety or ensure specifications are met.
Monitor work of contractors and subcontractors to ensure projects conform to plans and specifications.
Identify defects in materials, using non-destructive testing techniques, such as ultrasonic or radiographic testing.
Prepare reports on non-destructive testing results and document procedures.
Adjust or maintain equipment such as lasers, microscopes, or measurement devices.
Assist engineers in the development of new products, fixtures, tools, or processes.
Visually examine materials or components for signs of corrosion, cracks, or other flaws using various tools.

emerging_tasks.task: Operate, test, and troubleshoot uncrewed aerial systems, commonly known as drones, to ensure optimal performance.
Program and calibrate drones for specific missions or tasks, ensuring proper functionality and performance.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
